method: Density Based Active Learning for Data Streams
title: Density Based Active Learning for Data Streams
text_0: Idea: The Density Based Active Learning for Data Streams (DBALStream) query strategy is an extension to the uncertainty based query strategies proposed by Žliobaitė et al. In addition to the uncertainty assessment, StreamDensityBasedAL assesses the local density and only allows querying the label for a candidate if that local density is sufficiently high. The local density is represented by the number of other samples, the new sample is the new nearest neighbor to within a sliding window.

method: Cognitive Dual-Query Strategy with Random Sampling
title: Cognitive Dual-Query Strategy with Random Sampling
text_0: Idea: The Cognitive Dual-Query Strategy with Random Sampling query strategy is an extension to the uncertainty based query strategies proposed by Žliobaitė et al. and follows the same idea as DBALStream where querying a labels is only allowed if the local density around the corresponding sample is sufficiently high. The authors propose the use of a cognitive window that monitors the most representative samples within a data stream.
